edible_parts: Fruit (cooked — syrup, wine, pie) and flowers; raw fruit/stems toxic.
other_uses: Western elderberry with flat creamy flower clusters and powder-blue berry clusters.
prep: Cook the blue berries into syrup, jelly, wine, and pie and use the flowers for cordial and fritters — a major Western fruit; but the raw berries, and all green parts, stems, and seeds are toxic, so always cook and strain.
harvest: Berries late summer; flowers early summer.
risks: Raw berries, stems, leaves toxic — cook and strain.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
